title: Ricinocarpus diversifolius
intro:
There is no plant known as Ricinocarpus diversifolius. However, I found some information on Ricinocarpus succulentus and Ricinocarpus communis. However, I found more information on Ricinocarpus communis. 

Ricinocarpus communis, also known as Indian staff tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ricinocarpus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tonics, often used to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.

Ricinocarpus diversifolius is native to Australia, where it grows in the eastern and southern regions. The plant is classified within the genus Ricinocarpus, which is a part of the family Santalaceae.
